title: Extract guild member records from two-level XML wrapper (Greptile P1, re-review)
rationale: guild --all --members 1 returned wrapper objects instead of member records and ran to the 100-page cap. BGG nests guild members two levels deep: {"guild":{"members":{"@count":"N","member":[...]}}}. The previous extractPaginatedItems/loneChildObject path returned the whole members wrapper as a single item per page, and paginationTotalFromEnvelope only checked one level for @total (and didn't recognize @count), so the inferred page size was 1, the total stayed 0, and the full-page heuristic advanced until the 100-page safety cap, surfacing wrapper objects rather than members.

changes.summary: extractArrayItems now unwraps a named collection field whose value is an XML wrapper object (e.g. members -> {@count, member:[...]}) via the new repeatedElementFromWrapper helper, returning individual member records (array, or a lone object for the BadgerFish single-item collapse). paginationTotalFromEnvelope now searches nested objects (depth-bounded) and totalFromAttributes recognizes @count, so guild's members/@count is used as the exact total.
changes.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; guild --all --members 1 collects all members and stops at @count instead of the 100-page cap; plays pagination unchanged.

changes.summary: Added TestPaginatedGetGuildMembersWalksAllPages (two-level wrapper, member records not wrapper objects, stops at @count), TestExtractPaginatedItemsGuildWrapper (multi + single-member collapse), TestPaginationTotalNestedCount (@count nested extraction).
changes.validated_outcome: go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/ passes including the new guild tests.
